title: ranunculus islandicus
intro: Ranunculus islandicus is a species of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) that is native to Iceland. It is also known as the Icelandic buttercup. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus islandicus can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and it is also used in some traditional medicine practices to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
